evals.prompt: I want to add BarcodeCapture to my Android app. Here's my empty Activity: EmptyActivity.kt. I need to scan EAN-13 and Code 128 barcodes for a retail app.
evals.expected_output: The skill reads integration.md, fetches the latest SDK version, writes complete BarcodeCapture integration code into EmptyActivity.kt (BarcodeCapture.forDataCaptureContext, BarcodeCaptureListener, Camera, DataCaptureView, BarcodeCaptureOverlay, onResume/onPause lifecycle), and shows the setup checklist (Gradle deps, AndroidManifest permissions, runtime permission, license key).

assertions.text: Setup checklist is shown and mentions adding com.scandit.datacapture:barcode and com.scandit.datacapture:core Gradle dependencies
Setup checklist mentions adding CAMERA permission to AndroidManifest.xml
Setup checklist mentions requesting CAMERA permission at runtime
A license key placeholder string is present
DataCaptureContext.forLicenseKey( is used to create the context
BarcodeCapture.createRecommendedCameraSettings() is used (not VideoResolution.AUTO)
Camera.getDefaultCamera( is used to obtain the camera
dataCaptureContext.setFrameSource( is called with the camera
BarcodeCaptureSettings is constructed and configured
Symbology.EAN13_UPCA is enabled
Symbology.CODE128 is enabled
No symbologies other than EAN13_UPCA and CODE128 are enabled
BarcodeCapture.forDataCaptureContext(dataCaptureContext, settings) is used to create the mode
barcodeCapture.addListener( is called
DataCaptureView.newInstance( is used to create the view
BarcodeCaptureOverlay.newInstance(barcodeCapture, dataCaptureView) is called
The Activity implements BarcodeCaptureListener
onBarcodeScanned is overridden and reads session.newlyRecognizedBarcode
onSessionUpdated, onObservationStarted, and onObservationStopped are overridden
barcodeCapture.isEnabled = false is called inside onBarcodeScanned
camera.switchToDesiredState(FrameSourceState.ON) is called in onResume (or equivalent)
camera.switchToDesiredState(FrameSourceState.OFF) is called in onPause (or equivalent)
barcodeCapture.removeListener( is called in onDestroy
dataCaptureContext.removeCurrentMode() is called in onDestroy

evals.prompt: Add barcode scanning to my existing Android Activity. Here's the Activity: EmptyActivity.kt. I want to scan QR codes and Data Matrix. When a barcode is scanned, print the barcode data to the log.
evals.expected_output: The skill integrates BarcodeCapture into EmptyActivity.kt with QR and Data Matrix symbologies enabled. onBarcodeScanned logs the barcode data using Log.d or similar. Shows the setup checklist.

assertions.text: Symbology.QR is enabled
Symbology.DATA_MATRIX is enabled
No symbologies other than QR and DATA_MATRIX are enabled
BarcodeCapture.forDataCaptureContext( is used
onBarcodeScanned is implemented and accesses barcode.data
The scan result is logged (Log.d, println, or similar)
onResume turns the camera on
onPause turns the camera off
Setup checklist mentions Gradle dependencies

evals.prompt: When a barcode is scanned in my Android BarcodeCapture app, I want to disable scanning, look up the barcode in a remote API (a suspend function / coroutine call), and re-enable scanning when the lookup completes. Wire this up safely.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets barcodeCapture.isEnabled = false at the start of onBarcodeScanned, launches a coroutine for the async lookup, and re-enables scanning when the coroutine completes. Uses lifecycleScope or similar so the coroutine is tied to the Activity lifecycle.

assertions.text: barcodeCapture.isEnabled = false is set inside onBarcodeScanned before the async work
A coroutine (launch, async) or runOnUiThread with post is used to perform the async lookup
barcodeCapture.isEnabled = true is set after the lookup completes
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, barcodeCapture, camera, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: I want to suppress the default beep and vibration when a barcode is scanned in my Android app — silent scanning. Update the BarcodeCapture feedback.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets barcodeCapture.feedback to a BarcodeCaptureFeedback with no sound or vibration (either the empty constructor or by assigning null/empty Feedback to the success property).

assertions.text: barcodeCapture.feedback is reassigned to suppress audio and haptic feedback (BarcodeCaptureFeedback() empty constructor or equivalent)
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, barcodeCapture, camera, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: Add a square viewfinder to my Android BarcodeCapture preview so users can see where to aim.
evals.expected_output: The skill creates a RectangularViewfinder with SQUARE style and assigns it to overlay.viewfinder. The overlay is the BarcodeCaptureOverlay created in the Activity.

assertions.text: A RectangularViewfinder is constructed with a SQUARE style (or equivalent)
The viewfinder is assigned to a BarcodeCaptureOverlay's viewfinder property (overlay.viewfinder = ...)
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, barcodeCapture, camera, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: I need to filter scans so duplicates of the same code within 500 milliseconds are ignored. Update my BarcodeCapture settings.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets codeDuplicateFilter = 500 on the BarcodeCaptureSettings instance before creating the BarcodeCapture mode (or applies new settings via applySettings).

assertions.text: codeDuplicateFilter is set to 500 (milliseconds) on the BarcodeCaptureSettings
codeDuplicateFilter is set before BarcodeCapture.forDataCaptureContext( or via applySettings
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, barcodeCapture, camera, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: Clean up my BarcodeCapture Android integration properly when the Activity is destroyed — remove the listener, remove the mode from the context, and turn off the camera.
evals.expected_output: The skill ensures onDestroy calls barcodeCapture.removeListener(this) and dataCaptureContext.removeCurrentMode(), and that onPause calls camera.switchToDesiredState(FrameSourceState.OFF).

assertions.text: onDestroy calls barcodeCapture.removeListener(this) or equivalent
onDestroy calls dataCaptureContext.removeCurrentMode() or removeAllModes()
onPause (or onDestroy) calls camera.switchToDesiredState(FrameSourceState.OFF)
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, barcodeCapture, camera, listener) is preserved
